threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think saying 'hello' to strangers on the street is normal. Here, it’s just a nod and keep moving.
Caught a late 333 last week; the driver had a full-blown karaoke session with a group of tired clubbers. Best show of the night!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I spotted that old lady at the corner bakery, always haggling over a penny and leaving with a smile. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn stops seem to have a soundtrack? There's always someone playing a sax or a violin, like it’s a concert down there.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local when they dodge the dog on the tracks like it’s second nature, while newcomers just stare in confusion.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 49 during rush hour—three people singing 'We Are Family' while the bus crawled past a street musician. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a paella in Gràcia like on the beach. Most locals wouldn’t touch the stuff here. We're all about the tapas!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their puzzlement at the lack of a lift in many buildings - few locals bat an eye at the stairs to their flats.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' instead of 'the Chocolate Line' like the locals do. Get ready for some confused looks on the platform.
Last night on the 196, a guy in a parka kept trying to bargain with the driver for a free ride. Never seen that before.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in full lederhosen ordering a döner at 3 AM, I realized Neukölln really is a living tapestry of surprises.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a bar that serves currywurst cocktails—spicy, tangy, and a total conversation starter.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their casual dismissal of BART delays—while newcomers are still looking for updates on their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy fixing his sax on the bus, played a sweet tune just as the doors opened. It was like a mini concert before the chaos outside.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll through Parc Güell without realizing it’s often packed and the locals avoid it like the plague.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their bewildered look trying to navigate the Gràcia street art while fumbling with a paper map instead of using Google Maps.
